Package org.solenopsis.soap.tooling

Class RunTestsResult

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.RunTestsResult
public class RunTestsResult extends Object

Java class for RunTestsResult complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="RunTestsResult">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="apexLogId" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="codeCoverage" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}CodeCoverageResult" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="codeCoverageWarnings" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}CodeCoverageWarning" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="failures" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}RunTestFailure" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="flowCoverage" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowCoverageResult" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="flowCoverageWarnings" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowCoverageWarning" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="numFailures" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int"/>
         <element name="numTestsRun" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int"/>
         <element name="successes" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}RunTestSuccess" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="totalTime" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}double"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getApexLogId

      public String getApexLogId()
      Gets the value of the apexLogId property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setApexLogId

      public void setApexLogId(String value)
      Sets the value of the apexLogId property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getCodeCoverage

      public List<CodeCoverageResult> getCodeCoverage()
      Gets the value of the codeCoverage property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the codeCoverage property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCodeCoverage().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CodeCoverageResult

      Returns:
      The value of the codeCoverage property.

    • getCodeCoverageWarnings

      public List<CodeCoverageWarning> getCodeCoverageWarnings()
      Gets the value of the codeCoverageWarnings property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the codeCoverageWarnings property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCodeCoverageWarnings().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CodeCoverageWarning

      Returns:
      The value of the codeCoverageWarnings property.

    • getFailures

      public List<RunTestFailure> getFailures()
      Gets the value of the failures property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the failures property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getFailures().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list RunTestFailure

      Returns:
      The value of the failures property.

      public List<FlowCoverageResult> getFlowCoverage()
      Gets the value of the flowCoverage property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the flowCoverage property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getFlowCoverage().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowCoverageResult

      Returns:
      The value of the flowCoverage property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the flowCoverageWarnings property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getFlowCoverageWarnings().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowCoverageWarning

      The value of the flowCoverageWarnings property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the successes property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getSuccesses().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list RunTestSuccess
